item.title: A quick overview of the tools I use in general
item.description: HOI So this is just a small list of things I use in my daily life…. I don't really use the computer to game much so IDK what to put in as my gaming software. I mainly write my code in the terminal with either neovim or helix (Shoutout silt) or Emacs for taking notes and such. In fact this site was made using Org mode! I have a pretty good Neovim config I am proud of.

item.title: A fresh begining with rust
item.description: Hoi.. How did we get here…. So recently I was wondering what it'd be like to use Rust. IDK why….I just had the urge to try rust. Then something weird happened. I saw a No boilerplate video where they made a rust-only based command line envirorment. So zellij for the terminal multiplexer, Nu for the shell, Helix for the editor among many others. That made me wonder what the future of the command line would look like.

item.title: A guide to making a small Gnu or busybox based linux distro from scratch
item.description: A guide to making a small Gnu or busybox based linux distro from scratch because people seem to think it is too hard. Why? The typical linux neckbeard stereotype ranking goes Arch then gentoo and then LFS(even though arch has become a "normie distro" due to the fact that the archinstall script now exists). There is an assumption that LFS is hard to build and configure. Well it is kinda true but also not really since the complexity only happens when you need to do some advanced configuration.

item.title: FKF’s about info
item.description: aaaaaaaaaa Hoi I Am FKF. I'm a random 19 year old programmer. Uhh…..IDK what else to say…… I like music…..but I suppose most people do. Though my taste in music is a bit…odd to say the least. I do like to read though. Mainly fiction…. I also like to read Korean comic books AKA Manhwas. Though nowadays I don't really have the time to read because I am busy often.

item.title: My (moderately small) journey with Org mode
item.description: So I recently randomly decided to try to use Emacs for my note taking because I heard a lot of good things about the org mode. And holy fucking fuck Org mode is amazing to say the least. It is more robust than markdown due to supporting much much more feature wise. Also the Emacs editor itself is also just a pleasure to work with( with Evil AKA vim key bindings of course since I don't want to fucking destroy my fingers ).
